sections.heading: Introduction
sections.text: Live 6 guides

sections.heading: System Configuration
sections.text: Partner setup, templates, bonus, events, and maintenance.

sections.heading: Scope and Purpose
sections.text: Use this page to understand what Back Office covers, who uses it, and how this documentation should be consumed.

sections.heading: Overview
sections.text: Playage Back Office is the central operational console for day-to-day platform administration. It brings operations, finance, support, reporting, and configuration workflows into one controlled environment.

sections.heading: Purpose
sections.text:
The Back Office exists to keep operations:
Reliable in real-time execution
Traceable through audit-ready workflows
Governed by role-based permissions
Scalable across teams and regions

sections.heading: Scope
sections.text:
This documentation covers the core BO domains:
Getting started and secure access
Player and user operations
Reporting and analytics modules
Financial reporting and reconciliation support
Operations/support handling workflows
System and admin-level configuration controls

sections.heading: Intended Audience
sections.text:
BO administrators and team leads
Operations and support teams
Finance and reconciliation teams
Compliance and risk reviewers
Product and configuration owners

sections.heading: How to Use This Guide
sections.text:
Start from the BO home section and choose your functional area.
Follow pages in the recommended order inside each section.
Use filter and validation checklists before exporting critical reports.
Apply troubleshooting steps before escalating.

sections.heading: Success Criteria
sections.text:
A reader should be able to:
Perform daily BO tasks with minimal confusion
Validate outputs before decision making
Escalate only when required, with complete context
Keep workflows consistent across teams

sections.heading: Best Practice
sections.text: Treat this documentation as an operational runbook: update it whenever process or policy changes happen.

sections.heading: Getting Started
sections.text: Use this section to complete the first Back Office access flow with the correct sequence and minimum risk.

sections.heading: Scope
sections.text:
This guide covers:
Login and authentication checks
Platform selection after login
First dashboard validation
Initial access verification for operations

sections.heading: Before You Start
sections.text:
Confirm you are using the correct environment URL (staging or production).
Ensure your account is active and assigned to the correct role.
Keep 2FA device/app ready if your account requires OTP.
Avoid shared browsers for admin access.

sections.heading: Recommended Reading Order
sections.text:
Login and Authentication
Administration Dashboard
Admin Back Office Dashboard

sections.heading: Expected Result
sections.text:
After completing this section, you should be able to:
Sign in successfully
Open Back Office from the platform selector
Validate key dashboard widgets and menu visibility
Confirm your role-based access is correct

sections.heading: Common Issues During Onboarding
sections.text:
Login URL opens wrong environment : verify domain and saved bookmarks.
Backoffice card is missing : role permission or module mapping is incomplete.
Partial menu visibility : account policy changed and requires re-login.

sections.heading: Best Practice
sections.text:
Keep this section as a standard onboarding checklist for every new admin.
Re-run this checklist after role updates, policy changes, or environment switch.

sections.heading: Login and Authentication
sections.text: Use this flow to sign in securely and verify your Back Office session is valid.

sections.heading: When to Use
sections.text:
First login of the day
New user onboarding
Access verification after password or role changes

sections.heading: Login Screen
sections.text:
Reference screenshot for documentation purposes:
https://userguide.playagegaming.tech/img/bo/getting-started/2-1-1/dewli5h8qbniz923-embedded-image-lomvc7zx-8c8de41e.webp

sections.heading: Steps
sections.text:
Open the official Back Office URL.
Verify domain + https:// .
Enter registered email.
Enter password.
Click Sign In .
Complete OTP/2FA if prompted.
Wait for redirect to dashboard selector.

sections.heading: Field Notes
sections.text:
Email : Must match the admin account.
Password : Case-sensitive.
Forgot Password : Use reset flow if enabled.
Reference screenshot for documentation purposes:
https://userguide.playagegaming.tech/img/bo/getting-started/2-1-1/czcrnluygh01siwi-image-27b2fd6a.webp

sections.heading: Expected Result
sections.text:
Account opens without errors.
Correct modules are visible for your role.
Session remains stable (no instant logout).

sections.heading: Troubleshooting
sections.text:
Invalid credentials : Check keyboard layout, caps, and email typo.
2FA failure : Sync device time and generate a fresh OTP.
Account locked : Request unlock from system admin.
Redirect loop : Clear site cookies and sign in again.

sections.heading: Security Tips
sections.text:
Never share password/OTP.
Avoid shared or unmanaged devices.
Always sign out after sensitive operations.

sections.heading: Administration Dashboard
sections.text: After login, this page lets you choose which platform module to open.

sections.heading: Purpose
sections.text: Use this step to enter Back Office with the correct role context.

sections.heading: Platform Selector
sections.text:
Reference screenshot for documentation purposes:
https://userguide.playagegaming.tech/img/bo/getting-started/2-2-1/qqfg9v2od29ghn08-embedded-image-kqeemioc-fea0e76e.webp

sections.heading: Card Overview
sections.text:
Back Office : Users, operations, finance, reports, settings.
CMS : Content, media, and publishing workflows.
BAS : KPI and analytics insights.

sections.heading: Steps to Open Back Office
sections.text:
Locate the Backoffice card.
Confirm access is available for your user role.
Click Access Dashboard .
Wait for redirect to BO dashboard.
Reference screenshot for documentation purposes:
https://userguide.playagegaming.tech/img/bo/getting-started/2-2-1/0rvxfpk6sgkdcjen-image-0b94e834.webp

sections.heading: Validation
sections.text:
BO dashboard loads fully.
Expected menu groups are visible.
Restricted modules remain hidden.

sections.heading: Troubleshooting
sections.text:
Card missing : Module not assigned to role.
Card not opening : Session expired or browser blocking scripts.
Permission error after open : Role policy updated; re-sync access.

sections.heading: Best Practice
sections.text:
Keep least-privilege role mapping.
Use separate accounts when BO and CMS duties differ.

sections.heading: Admin Back Office Dashboard
sections.text: This page explains how to read and validate the main BO dashboard at shift start.

sections.heading: Purpose
sections.text:
Monitor high-level KPIs
Confirm daily operations are healthy
Navigate quickly to detail modules

sections.heading: Dashboard Overview
sections.text:
Reference screenshot for documentation purposes:
https://userguide.playagegaming.tech/img/bo/getting-started/2-3-1/KI0zPgivP3F0dU7v-image.webp

Switch between major modules from top tabs.
Open child pages from dropdown menus.
Return to dashboard via logo/home.

sections.heading: Date Filters
sections.text:

sections.heading: KPI Cards
sections.text:
Reference screenshot for documentation purposes:
https://userguide.playagegaming.tech/img/bo/getting-started/2-3-1/is0ycx8uadmflevb-image-f9e645e7.webp

Track:
Deposits and withdrawals
Active users and traffic movement
Profit/balance indicators
Bonus impact

sections.heading: Troubleshooting
sections.text:
Stale widgets : Re-apply filter + refresh page.
Mismatch with reports : Check same date range/timezone.
Missing modules : Verify role permissions and re-login.

sections.heading: Best Practice
sections.text: Run a short dashboard validation at the start of each shift before taking operations actions.

sections.heading: Player Management and Analytics
sections.text: This page is used to monitor player accounts, activity, and operational player data.

sections.heading: Screen Layout
sections.text:
Reference screenshot for documentation purposes:
https://userguide.playagegaming.tech/img/bo/player-management/3-1-1/qddjrkg0gvfepewm-image-43595ed3.webp
Main areas:
Filters and search controls
Refresh action for data load
Player data table
Pagination and result controls

sections.heading: Standard Workflow
sections.text:
Open Player Management dashboard.
Apply required filters (date, segment, status).
Click Refresh to load latest records.
Review player table and activity indicators.

sections.heading: Refresh and Data Loading
sections.text:
Reference screenshot for documentation purposes:
https://userguide.playagegaming.tech/img/bo/player-management/3-1-1/xuqwloklrrrtrwng-image-4b1aa2b4.webp
If table is empty on first load, use Refresh .
Refresh pulls updated data from API/server.
Re-run refresh after changing filter criteria.

sections.heading: Player Table Review
sections.text:
Reference screenshot for documentation purposes:
https://userguide.playagegaming.tech/img/bo/player-management/3-1-1/qv8ymt4fubthm5yu-image-eeed3c14.webp
Typical fields include:
Player ID and username
Verification state
Contact and profile data
Login/activity markers
Balance indicators

sections.heading: Troubleshooting
sections.text:
No data after refresh : Clear filters and retry with wider date range.
Slow table loading : Reduce date span and use narrower filters.
Unexpected player values : Verify active currency/filter settings.

sections.heading: Best Practice
sections.text:
Filter first, then refresh for large datasets.
Review anomalies (login spikes, status changes) daily.
Keep access audits aligned with compliance requirements.

sections.heading: Player Document Management
sections.text: This page is used to review uploaded player documents and take compliance actions.

sections.heading: Screen Layout
sections.text:
Reference screenshot for documentation purposes:
https://userguide.playagegaming.tech/img/bo/player-management/3-1-2/ejkdgonvjdnf1cri-image-da210254.webp
Core areas:
Document list table
Document status fields
Action column with view/review options

sections.heading: Open Document Details
sections.text:
Find target row in document table.
Click View (eye icon) from actions.
Review document details and metadata.

sections.heading: Document Preview and Actions
sections.text:
Reference screenshot for documentation purposes:
https://userguide.playagegaming.tech/img/bo/player-management/3-1-2/kw6zxiirj1smhkht-image-af207d3e.webp
Possible actions (based on role/status):
Verify/review details
Reject document for re-verification
Delete document (authorized roles only)

sections.heading: Typical Use Cases
sections.text:
KYC verification checks
Document quality and mismatch review
Compliance review before approval

sections.heading: Troubleshooting
sections.text:
View popup not opening : Check row permissions and retry after refresh.
Status not updated : Confirm action saved and reload table.
Wrong player document shown : Validate selected row and document ID.

sections.heading: Best Practice
sections.text:
Use reject with clear reason for audit clarity.
Restrict delete actions to limited admin roles.

sections.heading: Player Performance and Conversion Analytics
sections.text: This report measures conversion performance from registration to deposit activity.

sections.heading: Conversion Report Screen
sections.text:
Reference screenshot for documentation purposes:
https://userguide.playagegaming.tech/img/bo/player-management/3-1-3/siq8vu2tigghkdat-image-918bc33c.webp
Main metrics:
Registered users
Depositing users
Deposit count and total amount
Average deposit amount

sections.heading: Workflow
sections.text:
Open conversion report page.
Apply filters (date range, currency, source).
Review conversion metrics and trends.
Export filtered results if required.

sections.heading: Export Options
sections.text:
CSV for quick data sharing/integration.
XLS for spreadsheet analysis.

sections.heading: Typical Use Cases
sections.text:
Campaign conversion measurement
Deposit behavior comparison
Segment-level conversion tracking

sections.heading: Troubleshooting
sections.text:
Metrics look incorrect : Recheck filters and active date range.
Export missing rows : Confirm table is fully filtered as intended.
Numbers differ from finance report : Validate report scope and timezone.

sections.heading: Best Practice
sections.text:
Lock a standard filter set for recurring monthly comparisons.
Document the export criteria used in decision reports.

sections.heading: Player KPI Reporting
sections.text: This page provides KPI-level player performance reporting in tabular format.

sections.heading: KPI Screen
sections.text:
Reference screenshot for documentation purposes:
https://userguide.playagegaming.tech/img/bo/player-management/3-1-4/g9ydce6rcaox4j2o-image-a09485bf.webp
Common KPI data:
Player identity and verification
Betting totals and stake values
Settled/unsettled metrics
Multi-currency totals

sections.heading: Workflow
sections.text:
Open KPI reporting page.
Apply optional filters.
Review KPI rows and outliers.
Export report for analysis.

sections.heading: Export
sections.text:
CSV for system-to-system usage.
XLS for operations/finance review.

sections.heading: Typical Use Cases
sections.text:
Verified player behavior comparison
KPI trend review by period
Internal analytics and performance packs

sections.heading: Troubleshooting
sections.text:
KPI rows missing : Check verification/status/date filters.
Slow report load : Reduce range and split queries by segment.
Mismatch with dashboard totals : Confirm same time window and filters.

sections.heading: Best Practice
sections.text:
Use consistent reporting windows for KPI comparisons.
Keep export rights limited to analytics/admin roles.

sections.heading: Self-Excluded Client Management
sections.text: This page is used to manage responsible gaming self-exclusion cases.

sections.heading: Self-Exclusion Screen
sections.text:
Reference screenshot for documentation purposes:
https://userguide.playagegaming.tech/img/bo/player-management/3-1-5/yxm2kvu2hsmgdnsa-image-fc72e659.webp
Main capabilities:
View active and archived excluded clients
Filter by user/type/period
Track status and expiry timeline
Export records for compliance review

sections.heading: Standard Workflow
sections.text:
Open self-exclusion module.
Apply filters for target case set.
Review status, reason, and expiry timeline.
Process reactivation only after policy checks.

sections.heading: Compliance Handling
sections.text:
Keep complete audit trail for each case update.
Apply role-based control for modification actions.
Use dual approval where required by policy.

sections.heading: Troubleshooting
sections.text:
Client still active after exclusion : Trigger sync and verify policy application.
Case not visible : Expand filters and check status tab.
Reactivation blocked : Confirm mandatory approvals are completed.

sections.heading: Best Practice
sections.text:
Run periodic expiry reviews.
Archive exports for regulatory audits.

sections.heading: User List
sections.text: The User List page is used to create, update, and control Back Office users.

sections.heading: Screen Layout
sections.text:
Reference screenshot for documentation purposes:
https://userguide.playagegaming.tech/img/bo/user-management/4-1-1/x7nbphctczq70dbn-image-0bd7c7e2.webp
Main areas:
Search box (name, email, username)
Status filters
User table
Row actions (edit/deactivate/details)
Add New button

sections.heading: Add New User
sections.text:
Reference screenshot for documentation purposes:
https://userguide.playagegaming.tech/img/bo/user-management/4-1-1/l13wardh6f3remhn-image-160eae25.webp
https://userguide.playagegaming.tech/img/bo/user-management/4-1-1/xfmad3gdhf9sfbdl-image-584e3d9c.webp
Click Add New .
Enter username/email and password.
Fill first name and last name.
Optional: enable Must Change Password .
Click Save .

sections.heading: Assign Role and Permissions
sections.text:
Reference screenshot for documentation purposes:
https://userguide.playagegaming.tech/img/bo/user-management/4-1-1/0ruyjsott5d7f3mn-image-6cdf7280.webp
https://userguide.playagegaming.tech/img/bo/user-management/4-1-1/q0uho9gtxa5jjgeo-image-d45eca45.webp
Open the user from row actions.
Select required role.
Update module permissions.
Save changes.

sections.heading: Common Admin Tasks
sections.text:
Edit profile details for existing users.
Deactivate users who should not access the system.
Review role scope after organizational changes.

sections.heading: Troubleshooting
sections.text:
User not visible in table : Clear filters and search by exact username/email.
Save button does not create user : Check required fields and duplicate username.
User cannot access expected module : Recheck role assignment and permission toggles.

sections.heading: Best Practice
sections.text:
Apply least-privilege role design.
Keep 2FA enabled for high-privilege users.
Review inactive users regularly.

sections.heading: Role and Permission Management
sections.text: This page controls role creation and access distribution across Back Office modules.

sections.heading: Screen Layout
sections.text:
Reference screenshot for documentation purposes:
https://userguide.playagegaming.tech/img/bo/user-management/4-1-2/vstgfzsde8w8uets-image-9502669b.webp
You can review:
Role name
Assigned user count
Last update time
Edit/Delete actions

sections.heading: Create a New Role
sections.text:
Reference screenshot for documentation purposes:
https://userguide.playagegaming.tech/img/bo/user-management/4-1-2/aztqpjq6a5nktpa2-image-294c52ea.webp
https://userguide.playagegaming.tech/img/bo/user-management/4-1-2/hljv0w5lsk33ogjh-embedded-image-pjkos1ts-edaf7202.webp
Click Add Role .
Enter role name.
Enable permissions by module.
Save role.

sections.heading: Edit or Delete Role
sections.text:
Edit : update module permissions or role scope.
Delete : allowed only when no users are assigned.

sections.heading: Recommended Workflow
sections.text:
Create role first.
Test role on one user account.
Confirm module visibility.
Roll out to remaining users.

sections.heading: Troubleshooting
sections.text:
Role not assignable in user form : Refresh page after role creation.
Permission change not reflected : Re-login affected user session.
Delete role blocked : Remove linked users first.

sections.heading: Best Practice
sections.text:
Keep roles business-function based (Support, Finance, Compliance).
Avoid direct ad-hoc permission changes without role review.

sections.heading: Login History
sections.text: Use this page to monitor login behavior, failed attempts, and security access patterns.

sections.heading: Login History Screen
sections.text:
Reference screenshot for documentation purposes:
https://userguide.playagegaming.tech/img/bo/user-management/4-1-3/0und0azqvmme3xya-image-7efe1475.webp
Core columns:
Username
Login time
Logout time
IP address
Browser/device
Status (success, failed, blocked)

sections.heading: How to Audit Login Activity
sections.text:
Filter by date range.
Filter by status ( Failed / Blocked ) for risk checks.
Review repeated attempts from same IP.
Export selected records for compliance archive.

sections.heading: Typical Use Cases
sections.text:
Security review after suspicious login reports.
Compliance export for periodic audit.
Troubleshooting user access complaints.

sections.heading: Troubleshooting
sections.text:
No records after filtering : Expand date range and clear status filter.
Wrong timezone interpretation : Confirm server/report timezone standard.
User says login failed but no logs : Verify correct username and environment.

sections.heading: Best Practice
sections.text:
Restrict this page to admin/compliance users.
Investigate repeated failed attempts quickly.
Keep periodic exported snapshots for audit trail.

sections.heading: Checked Documents by User
sections.text: This report tracks user-level access to sensitive player documents.

sections.heading: Document Access Screen
sections.text:
Reference screenshot for documentation purposes:
https://userguide.playagegaming.tech/img/bo/user-management/4-1-4/c9jzrcej1k2hxnkf-image-2c97b47f.webp
Available controls:
User filter
Document-type filter
Date-range filter
Export (CSV/XLS)

sections.heading: Reporting Workflow
sections.text:
Select required date range.
Filter by user or document type.
Review access actions (viewed/downloaded/verified).
Export report for audit or compliance team.

sections.heading: When to Use This Report
sections.text:
Internal access-control review.
Compliance checks on document handling.
Investigation of unusual document access events.

sections.heading: Troubleshooting
sections.text:
Report looks empty : Clear filters and rerun with wider date range.
Export file missing rows : Verify active filters before download.
Unexpected access entries : Cross-check with login history and role permissions.

sections.heading: Best Practice
sections.text:
Use monthly exports for compliance archive.
Review high-sensitivity document access with security team.

sections.heading: Sports Book Bets Report
sections.text: Use this report for bet-level sportsbook analysis.

sections.heading: Interface Reference
sections.text:
Reference screenshot for documentation purposes:
https://userguide.playagegaming.tech/img/bo/reporting-and-analytics/5-1-1/a1rwa96uqkyp3kgt-image-75d0dee9.webp

sections.heading: Purpose
sections.text:
Review individual and aggregated bet performance
Monitor stake, payout, and margin behavior
Detect unusual bet patterns early

sections.heading: Steps
sections.text:
Open Reports > Sports Book > Bets Report .
Apply filters (date range, sport, market type, currency, status).
Review summary KPIs and detailed rows.
Drill into high-risk or high-value bets if needed.

sections.heading: Key Metrics
sections.text:
Bet count
Total stake
Win amount / payout
Gross win / margin
Active bettors

sections.heading: Export
sections.text:
Export after finalizing filters.
Use CSV/XLS for reconciliation.
Use PDF for management sharing.

sections.heading: Troubleshooting
sections.text:
Data mismatch : check timezone and same filter set.
No rows returned : broaden date range and remove strict filters.
Slow load : narrow the range and split analysis by sport.

sections.heading: Best Practice
sections.text: Start with a broad daily range, then drill down by sport/market to identify variance drivers.

sections.heading: Sports Book Sports Report
sections.text: Use this report to compare sportsbook performance by sport category.

sections.heading: Interface Reference
sections.text:
Reference screenshot for documentation purposes:
https://userguide.playagegaming.tech/img/bo/reporting-and-analytics/5-1-2/b4dkpehdngg4wwfk-image-d35e8750.webp

sections.heading: Purpose
sections.text:
Compare football, basketball, and other sports performance
Identify top and underperforming sport segments
Support event-level and campaign-level decisions

sections.heading: Steps
sections.text:
Open Reports > Sports Book > Sports Report .
Select date range, provider, sport, and optional bonus filters.
Apply filters and review totals.
Compare trends across sports before exporting.

sections.heading: Key Metrics
sections.text:
Bet volume per sport
Stake and payout by sport
Gross gaming revenue (GGR)
Profit/loss contribution

sections.heading: Export
sections.text:
Export in CSV/XLS for deeper analysis.
Use PDF for snapshot reporting.

sections.heading: Troubleshooting
sections.text:
Unexpected low volume : verify selected provider and currency.
Totals differ from bet report : align status and date filters.
Partial visibility : role access may restrict certain sports/providers.

sections.heading: Best Practice
sections.text: Use this page with Bets Report together: first find anomaly at sport level, then validate at bet level.

sections.heading: Sportsbook Competitions Report
sections.text: Use this report for competition-level sportsbook monitoring.

sections.heading: Interface Reference
sections.text:
Reference screenshot for documentation purposes:
https://userguide.playagegaming.tech/img/bo/reporting-and-analytics/5-1-3/l2y8z8d0m9pk8sfe-image-12865330.webp

sections.heading: Purpose
sections.text:
Measure betting performance per competition
Track user engagement across leagues/events
Evaluate competition-level profitability

sections.heading: Steps
sections.text:
Open Reports > Sports Book > Competitions Report .
Apply date range, sport, competition, provider, and currency filters.
Review competition KPIs and ranking.
Export selected view for operations/audit.

sections.heading: Key Metrics
sections.text:
Bets and turnover per competition
Win/payout values
Margin and net contribution
User participation trend

sections.heading: Export
sections.text:
Export competition snapshots for weekly planning.
Keep filter presets consistent across periods for fair comparison.

sections.heading: Troubleshooting
sections.text:
No expected competition : check date window and sport filter.
Performance spikes : verify if promo/event period overlaps.
Export differs from screen : refresh and re-apply filters before export.

sections.heading: Best Practice
sections.text: Review by short periods (daily/weekly) to detect sudden competition volatility early.

sections.heading: Sportsbook Market Analysis
sections.text: Use this report to analyze sportsbook market-type behavior.

sections.heading: Interface Reference
sections.text:
Reference screenshot for documentation purposes:
https://userguide.playagegaming.tech/img/bo/reporting-and-analytics/5-1-4/eetrzhszw9clqhoz-image-12d676a7.webp

sections.heading: Purpose
sections.text:
Compare market types by turnover and profitability
Identify high-risk/high-margin market segments
Support odds and exposure control decisions

sections.heading: Steps
sections.text:
Open Reports > Sports Book > Market Analysis .
Select date range and market-type filters.
Apply provider/sport/currency filters as needed.
Compare market-level outcomes and export.

sections.heading: Key Metrics
sections.text:
Bet count by market type
Stake, payout, and net margin
Win ratio distribution
Market-level volatility indicators

sections.heading: Export
sections.text:
Use XLS/CSV for market-level modeling.
Export PDF for summary communication.

sections.heading: Troubleshooting
sections.text:
Missing market types : role scope or sport filter may limit results.
High variance day-to-day : compare equivalent time windows only.
Load issues : reduce date range and disable non-essential filters.

sections.heading: Best Practice
sections.text: Standardize filter sets for recurring market reviews to maintain comparable trends.

sections.heading: Casino Report by Game
sections.text: Use this report to evaluate performance at game level.

sections.heading: Interface Reference
sections.text:
Reference screenshot for documentation purposes:
https://userguide.playagegaming.tech/img/bo/reporting-and-analytics/5-2-1/ttnxx7oocvxwuuvp-image-ca616812.webp

sections.heading: Purpose
sections.text:
Identify top and low-performing games
Track turnover, win, and margin by game
Support game-level optimization decisions

sections.heading: Steps
sections.text:
Open Reports > Casino > Report by Game .
Apply date, provider, game category, and game filters.
Review game-level KPIs.
Sort by turnover/margin to prioritize analysis.

sections.heading: Key Metrics
sections.text:
Bets and turnover per game
Payout and net revenue
Margin trend
Active player count

sections.heading: Export
sections.text:
Export filtered dataset in CSV/XLS for deeper analysis.
Use PDF for quick summaries.

sections.heading: Troubleshooting
sections.text:
Game not listed : check provider, category, and date filters.
Totals mismatch : align status/date/timezone across reports.
Large dataset : split by provider or category.

sections.heading: Best Practice
sections.text: Review top games daily and long-tail games weekly to balance speed and depth.

sections.heading: Casino Report by Partner
sections.text: Use this report to compare casino performance across partners.

sections.heading: Interface Reference
sections.text:
Reference screenshot for documentation purposes:
https://userguide.playagegaming.tech/img/bo/reporting-and-analytics/5-2-2/gx3buyvbwqh6trsj-image-af12936a.webp

sections.heading: Purpose
sections.text:
Measure partner-level business contribution
Compare profitability and activity across partner segments
Support account management and partner reviews

sections.heading: Steps
sections.text:
Open Reports > Casino > Report by Partner .
Apply date, partner, provider, and currency filters.
Compare partner KPIs.
Export partner snapshots for review meetings.

sections.heading: Key Metrics
sections.text:
Bet volume by partner
Turnover and payout totals
Net revenue and margin
Active users by partner

sections.heading: Export
sections.text:
Use CSV/XLS for partner reconciliation.
Use PDF for stakeholder sharing.

sections.heading: Troubleshooting
sections.text:
Partner missing : verify partner assignment and filter scope.
Low numbers : check timezone and date interval.
Access denied : role may not include all partner scopes.

sections.heading: Best Practice
sections.text: Keep a fixed weekly partner filter preset to track trend consistency.

sections.heading: Casino Report by Player
sections.text: Use this report for player-level casino analysis.

sections.heading: Interface Reference

sections.heading: Purpose
sections.text:
Monitor high-value and high-risk player behavior
Validate player profitability and betting patterns
Support risk/compliance reviews

sections.heading: Steps
sections.text:
Open Reports > Casino > Report by Player .
Filter by date, player, provider, and status.
Review player-level KPIs.
Drill into specific players if anomalies exist.

sections.heading: Key Metrics
sections.text:
Bets and turnover per player
Win/loss and net contribution
Bonus impact
Frequency/activity pattern

sections.heading: Export
sections.text:
Export selected player ranges for investigation.
Keep filter notes for repeatable audits.

sections.heading: Troubleshooting
sections.text:
Player not found : confirm player ID and selected date range.
Unexpected values : validate whether rollback/void bets are included.
Slow report : narrow search to targeted player segments.

sections.heading: Best Practice
sections.text: Use player report together with Player-Game report to confirm if variance is player-driven or game-driven.

sections.heading: Casino Report by Player-Game
sections.text: Use this report to analyze each player's activity per game.

Reference screenshot for documentation purposes:
https://userguide.playagegaming.tech/img/bo/reporting-and-analytics/5-2-4/ciath72qextuzygw-image-48b6a04b.webp

sections.heading: Purpose
sections.text:
Detect player-game concentration risks
Identify high-value player preferences
Compare game performance across player segments

sections.heading: Steps
sections.text:
Open Reports > Casino > Report by Player-Game .
Apply player, game, provider, and date filters.
Review bet/win/margin at player-game row level.
Export focused slices for fraud/risk/operations review.

sections.heading: Key Metrics
sections.text:
Bet count per player-game pair
Turnover and payout
Net result and margin
Engagement concentration

sections.heading: Export
sections.text:
Use CSV/XLS for model or audit workflows.
Export after confirming column and filter selections.

sections.heading: Troubleshooting
sections.text:
Too many rows : filter by player segment or specific games.
Mismatch with player report : align included statuses and date window.
No data : remove restrictive combined filters.

sections.heading: Best Practice
sections.text: Use this page when player-level spikes need root-cause at specific game level.

sections.heading: Casino Report by Provider
sections.text: Use this report to monitor casino provider performance.

Reference screenshot for documentation purposes:
https://userguide.playagegaming.tech/img/bo/reporting-and-analytics/5-2-5/ijlqfstbhej7l22h-image-729b6868.webp

sections.heading: Purpose
sections.text:
Compare provider contribution and efficiency
Measure turnover, payout, and margin per provider
Support provider-level operational decisions

sections.heading: Steps
sections.text:
Open Reports > Casino > Report by Provider .
Apply date, provider type, currency, and optional game filters.
Review provider rankings and trend movements.
Export provider report for finance/partnership review.

sections.heading: Key Metrics
sections.text:
Turnover by provider
Win/payout and net revenue
Margin contribution
Active player distribution

sections.heading: Export
sections.text:
Export in CSV/XLS for comparison sheets.
Use PDF for executive summary.

sections.heading: Troubleshooting
sections.text:
Provider totals look low : verify provider filter and date range.
Inconsistent margin : check if bonus/rollback adjustments are included.
Missing provider rows : role scope may limit visibility.

sections.heading: Best Practice
sections.text: Track providers on consistent weekly and monthly windows to separate noise from trend.

sections.heading: Casino Report by Bet
sections.text: Use this report for bet-level casino detail and anomaly checks.

sections.heading: Interface Reference
sections.text:
Reference screenshot for documentation purposes:
https://userguide.playagegaming.tech/img/bo/reporting-and-analytics/5-2-6/kaexfeyndvknl5z5-image-923ffe33.webp

sections.heading: Purpose
sections.text:
Inspect casino bets at transaction granularity
Verify payouts and margins on specific bet records
Support investigation and reconciliation workflows

sections.heading: Steps
sections.text:
Open Reports > Casino > Report by Bet .
Apply filters (date, provider, player, game, bet status).
Review detailed rows and suspicious patterns.
Export selected data for audit follow-up.

sections.heading: Key Metrics
sections.text:
Bet ID / transaction references
Stake and payout values
Net result per bet
Status history (where available)

sections.heading: Export
sections.text:
Prefer CSV/XLS for transaction review.
Keep a copy of filter criteria with exported file.

sections.heading: Troubleshooting
sections.text:
No output : widen date range and remove strict player/game filter.
Value mismatch : confirm status inclusion (settled/void/rollback).
Performance issue : split analysis into smaller date windows.

sections.heading: Best Practice
sections.text: Use bet-level report as final verification layer after game/provider summaries.

sections.heading: Purpose
sections.text:
Track live casino bet flow and operational health
Compare provider/category performance in live sessions
Export live-bet data for reconciliation and incident review

sections.heading: Steps
sections.text:
Open Reports > Live Casino > Bets Management .
Apply filters (provider, game category, game type, date range).
Review live bet table and summary values.
Export selected results.

sections.heading: Key Metrics
sections.text:
Live bet volume
Stake and win totals
Bonus impact (if enabled)
Provider and category contribution

sections.heading: Export
sections.text:
Use CSV/XLS for operational follow-up.
Use PDF for shift or incident reporting.

sections.heading: Troubleshooting
sections.text:
Live data delay : verify source sync and refresh interval.
Empty report : check if selected period has active live-casino activity.
Export blocked : verify browser download policy.

sections.heading: Best Practice
sections.text: Run this report at fixed intervals during peak hours for faster incident detection.

sections.heading: Financial Payments by Region
sections.text: Use this report to compare payment performance across regions/countries.

sections.heading: Interface Reference
sections.text:
Reference screenshot for documentation purposes:
https://userguide.playagegaming.tech/img/bo/financial-reports/6-1-1/yjllo4ub0b0l96oi-image-3fbfa51c.webp

sections.heading: Purpose
sections.text:
Identify strong and weak regional payment performance.
Detect regional anomalies in payment flow.
Support region-specific risk and operations planning.

sections.heading: Steps
sections.text:
Open Financial Reports > Payments by Region .
Select date range and payment method filters.
Apply region/country filters.
Review totals and region-wise comparison.
Export selected view for finance review.

sections.heading: Key Metrics
sections.text:
Total payment amount per region
Transaction count
Success/failure ratio
Net settlement trend

sections.heading: How to Read This Report
sections.text:
High volume + low success ratio usually indicates provider or compliance friction.
A sudden regional drop should be compared with prior 7-day and 30-day windows.
Compare same weekday ranges to reduce traffic pattern noise.

sections.heading: Troubleshooting
sections.text:
Region data missing : verify role scope and region filters.
Mismatch with global totals : check if all payment methods are included.
Unexpected spike/drop : validate timezone and maintenance window overlaps.

sections.heading: Best Practice
sections.text: Create a weekly baseline export per major region and compare against current week.

sections.heading: Deposit Report: View and Export
sections.text: Use this report to monitor incoming deposit transactions and conversion quality.

sections.heading: Interface Reference
sections.text:
Reference screenshot for documentation purposes:
https://userguide.playagegaming.tech/img/bo/financial-reports/6-1-2/gcjxv0g4wp2o8wpn-image-f0c66c4d.webp

sections.heading: Purpose
sections.text:
Track deposit volume and success trends.
Validate settlement consistency by provider/method.
Export deposit data for audit and reconciliation.

sections.heading: Steps
sections.text:
Open Financial Reports > Deposit Report .
Choose date range, currency, status, and provider filters.
Apply filters and review summary values.
Inspect failed or pending transactions.
Export as CSV/XLS/PDF based on use case.

sections.heading: Key Metrics
sections.text:
Deposit count
Gross deposit amount
Success/failure/pending breakdown
Average deposit size

sections.heading: Export Guidance
sections.text:
Use CSV/XLS for reconciliation and pivot analysis.
Use PDF for management snapshots.
Save filter context with exported file name.

sections.heading: Troubleshooting
sections.text:
Low conversion : review failure reasons by method/provider.
Pending too high : check settlement delay from gateway.
Export mismatch : refresh, re-apply filters, then export again.

sections.heading: Best Practice
sections.text: Review failed deposits daily and correlate with provider incidents.

sections.heading: Withdrawal Transaction Report
sections.text: Use this report to monitor outgoing withdrawals and approval quality.

sections.heading: Interface Reference
sections.text:
Reference screenshot for documentation purposes:
https://userguide.playagegaming.tech/img/bo/financial-reports/6-1-3/ikhgiidxw4luhjdf-image-78d37bdf.webp

sections.heading: Purpose
sections.text:
Track withdrawal volume, approval, and rejection patterns.
Validate processing speed and payout consistency.
Support fraud/risk review for high-risk withdrawals.

sections.heading: Steps
sections.text:
Open Financial Reports > Withdrawal Report .
Filter by date, status, method, and player segment.
Review approved/rejected/pending totals.
Investigate outliers (high-value, repeated failures, long pending).
Export report for operations or compliance follow-up.

sections.heading: Key Metrics
sections.text:
Withdrawal count and amount
Approval/rejection ratio
Average processing time
High-value withdrawal concentration

sections.heading: How to Use for Operations
sections.text:
Use pending-age checks to detect operational bottlenecks.
Compare rejection reasons by method to identify policy friction.
Monitor same-player repeated attempts for risk flags.

sections.heading: Troubleshooting
sections.text:
Pending queue increasing : check approval workflow delays.
Rejection spike : validate KYC/AML rule changes.
Totals differ from payout records : align status and settlement cut-off time.

sections.heading: Best Practice
sections.text: Set daily thresholds for pending age and rejection ratio, then trigger internal alerts.

sections.heading: Financial Sales Report
sections.text: Use this report for consolidated financial sales analysis over custom periods.

sections.heading: Interface Reference
sections.text:
Reference screenshot for documentation purposes:
https://userguide.playagegaming.tech/img/bo/financial-reports/6-1-4/ot8mqks2xp8sxre8-image-91f6fb21.webp

sections.heading: Purpose
sections.text:
Analyze revenue contribution by channel/product.
Compare sales trends against deposits and withdrawals.
Support finance planning and executive reporting.

sections.heading: Steps
sections.text:
Open Financial Reports > Financial Sales Report .
Select period, currency, and channel/provider filters.
Review revenue totals and trend lines.
Compare with previous period (WoW/MoM).
Export final view for finance deck.

sections.heading: Key Metrics
sections.text:
Gross sales/revenue
Net sales contribution
Channel/provider split
Period-over-period change (%)

sections.heading: Analysis Tips
sections.text:
Always compare equivalent periods (same weekdays or month length).
Correlate sharp sales movements with campaigns and payment events.
Use both amount and count metrics; amount alone can hide conversion shifts.

sections.heading: Troubleshooting
sections.text:
Trend looks inconsistent : check currency and timezone settings.
Gap vs daily report : confirm cut-off timing.
Unexpected negative movement : inspect rollback/refund effects.

sections.heading: Best Practice
sections.text: Use this report as your primary weekly and monthly finance baseline.

sections.heading: Daily Sales Report
sections.text: Use this report for day-by-day revenue monitoring and quick operational checks.

sections.heading: Interface Reference
sections.text:
Reference screenshot for documentation purposes:
https://userguide.playagegaming.tech/img/bo/financial-reports/6-1-5/xying5h3hq7yr34l-image-1dfe9b23.webp

sections.heading: Purpose
sections.text:
Track daily sales movement.
Detect same-day anomalies fast.
Validate if operations are aligned with expected targets.

sections.heading: Steps
sections.text:
Open Financial Reports > Daily Sales Report .
Select target date or short date window.
Apply channel/provider filters.
Review daily totals and variance vs previous day.
Export day summary if needed.

sections.heading: Key Metrics
sections.text:
Daily gross sales
Daily net sales
Daily transaction count
Day-over-day change

sections.heading: Operational Usage
sections.text:
Morning: verify prior-day closure health.
Midday: check partial-day trend vs expected run rate.
End-of-day: export final snapshot for internal handover.

sections.heading: Troubleshooting
sections.text:
Large day-to-day swings : verify campaign, outages, or settlement delays.
Daily total not final : check report cut-off and processing window.
Missing channel : ensure filter includes all channels.

sections.heading: Best Practice
sections.text: Use daily report for fast monitoring, then confirm with Financial Sales Report for strategic view.

sections.heading: Financial Balance Report
sections.text: Use this report to monitor balance position and liquidity movement.

sections.heading: Interface Reference
sections.text:
Reference screenshot for documentation purposes:
https://userguide.playagegaming.tech/img/bo/financial-reports/6-1-6/mnhfojwhfdmkn9sb-image-d13f77cc.webp

sections.heading: Purpose
sections.text:
Validate current balance status.
Track inflow/outflow impact on liquidity.
Support treasury and risk monitoring.

sections.heading: Steps
sections.text:
Open Financial Reports > Balance Report .
Select date range, currency, and wallet/account scope.
Review opening, movement, and closing balances.
Cross-check with deposit/withdrawal summaries.
Export reconciliation-ready data.

sections.heading: Key Metrics
sections.text:
Opening balance
Total inflow / outflow
Net movement
Closing balance

sections.heading: Reconciliation Flow
sections.text:
Opening balance + inflow - outflow should match closing balance.
If mismatch exists, verify pending, rollback, and adjustment entries.

sections.heading: Troubleshooting
sections.text:
Balance mismatch : re-check cut-off time and pending statuses.
Negative balance alert : verify delayed settlements and manual adjustments.
Unexpected currency movement : confirm selected wallet/currency scope.

sections.heading: Best Practice
sections.text: Run end-of-day balance validation and keep a signed export for audit trail.

sections.heading: Game Transaction Report
sections.text: Use this report to review game-related financial transactions at transaction level.

sections.heading: Interface Reference
sections.text:
Reference screenshot for documentation purposes:
https://userguide.playagegaming.tech/img/bo/financial-reports/6-2-1/5otiiphwvalgxpfy-image-29b5481f.webp

sections.heading: Purpose
sections.text:
Monitor game transaction flow and value distribution.
Investigate game-level transaction anomalies.
Support operations, risk, and finance investigations.

sections.heading: Steps
sections.text:
Open Financial Reports > Game Transaction Report .
Apply game, provider, player, date, and status filters.
Review transaction rows and summary totals.
Investigate suspicious patterns (repeats, reversals, high-value spikes).
Export selected subset for case follow-up.

sections.heading: Key Metrics
sections.text:
Transaction count
Bet and win values
Net game transaction result
Reversal/adjustment frequency

sections.heading: Investigation Tips
sections.text:
Compare same game across different providers where possible.
Check repeated failed/reversed entries for integration issues.
Correlate spikes with release/campaign timelines.

sections.heading: Troubleshooting
sections.text:
No rows found : relax combined filters and increase range.
Mismatch with casino reports : align status and settlement logic.
Too many rows : split by provider or game category.

sections.heading: Best Practice
sections.text: Use this report as a deep-dive tool after summary reports identify suspicious movement.

sections.heading: Financial Transactions
sections.text: Use this page as the primary monitor for incoming and outgoing financial activity.

sections.heading: Interface Reference
sections.text:
Reference screenshot for documentation purposes:
https://userguide.playagegaming.tech/img/bo/operations-and-support/7-1-1/0osm6xgjsd7c20pn-image-7e743930.webp

sections.heading: Purpose
sections.text:
Provide a single operational view of finance transactions.
Detect anomalies early (repeats, delays, unusual amounts).
Support handover and escalation with traceable records.

sections.heading: Typical Workflow
sections.text:
Open Operations > Financial Transactions .
Apply date, transaction type, status, and currency filters.
Sort by latest updates and pending age.
Review high-value and failed items first.
Export or tag records for escalation if required.

sections.heading: Key Fields to Validate
sections.text:
Transaction ID / reference ID
Type (deposit/withdrawal/adjustment)
Status (success, failed, pending, reversed)
Requested amount vs settled amount
Updated timestamp and processor/source

sections.heading: Validation Checklist
sections.text:
Pending queue is within allowed SLA.
Failed ratio is within normal baseline.
No sudden concentration from one method/provider.
No duplicate transaction references.

sections.heading: Escalation Criteria
sections.text:
Escalate when:
Pending transactions exceed operational SLA.
Multiple high-value failures happen in short window.
Same account triggers repeated abnormal pattern.

sections.heading: Troubleshooting
sections.text:
Data appears stale : refresh and reapply filters.
Missing transactions : check scope filters (date/currency/type).
Status mismatch : verify settlement source update timing.

sections.heading: Best Practice
sections.text: Use this page every shift start and shift end to maintain clean operational continuity.

sections.heading: Deposit Requests
sections.text: Use this page to process and monitor user deposit requests.

sections.heading: Interface Reference
sections.text:
Reference screenshot for documentation purposes:
https://userguide.playagegaming.tech/img/bo/operations-and-support/7-1-2/gqyfelocvgjbskqm-image-9ae4964c.webp

sections.heading: Purpose
sections.text:
Track deposit request lifecycle from creation to settlement.
Resolve pending and failed requests quickly.
Reduce user wait time and support ticket load.

sections.heading: Request Lifecycle
sections.text:
Common statuses:
Pending : awaiting gateway/processor callback or manual validation.
Approved/Success : amount posted successfully.
Failed/Rejected : request not completed.
Reversed : settled then reversed due to correction/chargeback logic.

sections.heading: Steps
sections.text:
Open Operations > Deposit Requests .
Filter by status, payment method, date range, and user.
Start with oldest pending requests.
Check processor response/error code.
Mark for retry/escalation when required.
Export unresolved queue for shift handover.

sections.heading: What to Check Before Escalation
sections.text:
User account status is active and verified.
Payment method is enabled and healthy.
Duplicate attempts are not caused by user refresh behavior.
Requested and received amount mismatch is documented.

sections.heading: Troubleshooting
sections.text:
High pending volume : verify payment gateway callback delays.
Repeated failures on one method : check provider incident panel.
Amount mismatch : validate fees/conversion rules.

sections.heading: Best Practice
sections.text: Maintain a pending-age bucket (0-15m, 15-60m, 60m+) and resolve oldest first.

sections.heading: Withdrawal Requests
sections.text: Use this page to review, approve, and monitor withdrawal requests safely.

sections.heading: Interface Reference
sections.text:
Reference screenshot for documentation purposes:
https://userguide.playagegaming.tech/img/bo/operations-and-support/7-1-3/xegzmny2me9pimmd-image-e4e26c76.webp

sections.heading: Purpose
sections.text:
Control payout flow quality and speed.
Enforce risk, KYC, and policy checks before approval.
Keep rejection rates transparent and explainable.

sections.heading: Processing Flow
sections.text:
Open Operations > Withdrawal Requests .
Filter by pending/approved/rejected and date range.
Prioritize oldest pending and high-value requests.
Validate KYC status, account flags, and payment destination.
Approve/reject with reason notes.
Track post-action settlement confirmation.

sections.heading: Required Checks Before Approval
sections.text:
KYC/verification level meets payout policy.
Account has no unresolved risk flags.
Withdrawal amount and frequency are within thresholds.
Destination account format and ownership checks pass.

sections.heading: Rejection Handling
sections.text:
When rejecting, include clear reason category:
KYC incomplete
Policy threshold exceeded
Risk/compliance block
Payment destination mismatch

sections.heading: Troubleshooting
sections.text:
Pending queue growth : review reviewer capacity and approval bottlenecks.
Rejection spike : verify if policy rules were updated.
User disputes after rejection : ensure detailed reason is logged.

sections.heading: Best Practice
sections.text: Use dual control for high-value withdrawals: one reviewer + one final approver.

sections.heading: Cash Desk Live Chat
sections.text: Use this page for real-time communication on urgent transaction issues.

sections.heading: Interface Reference
sections.text:
Reference screenshot for documentation purposes:
https://userguide.playagegaming.tech/img/bo/operations-and-support/7-2-1/kgtntkvc1fqyf6m7-image-9e07c9a4.webp

sections.heading: Purpose
sections.text:
Resolve active payment/withdrawal issues quickly.
Coordinate between support, operations, and cash desk teams.
Reduce response latency for high-impact incidents.

sections.heading: Standard Handling Flow
sections.text:
Open incoming chat and classify issue type.
Confirm user identity and transaction reference.
Verify issue in operations pages before replying.
Provide action + ETA (or escalation notice).
Close chat only after confirmation.

sections.heading: Priority Model
sections.text:
P1 : payment stuck, large-value issue, repeated system failure.
P2 : delayed processing, verification mismatch.
P3 : informational or non-blocking query.

sections.heading: Agent Response Checklist
sections.text:
Include ticket/reference ID in first response.
Share next action and expected timeline.
Avoid ambiguous statements; use policy-safe language.
Capture escalation reason if transferred.

sections.heading: Troubleshooting
sections.text:
Chat not updating : refresh socket/session.
Wrong queue assignment : re-route to correct team queue.
Missing transaction context : request reference ID before action.

sections.heading: Best Practice
sections.text: Use short, structured responses: status, action, ETA, next update point.

sections.heading: Support Tickets
sections.text: Use this page to manage non-real-time user issues and internal follow-ups.

sections.heading: Interface Reference
sections.text:
Reference screenshot for documentation purposes:
https://userguide.playagegaming.tech/img/bo/operations-and-support/7-2-2/ssao6nptjixugph7-image-4bde09fa.webp

sections.heading: Purpose
sections.text:
Track issues end-to-end with ownership.
Ensure SLA compliance by status and priority.
Maintain documented audit trail of support actions.

sections.heading: Ticket Workflow
sections.text:
Open : ticket received and unassigned.
In Progress : agent accepted and is working.
Waiting : waiting for user/other team response.
Resolved/Closed : issue completed with resolution note.

sections.heading: Steps
sections.text:
Open Support > Tickets .
Filter by status, priority, and queue.
Assign ticket owner.
Add resolution notes with references.
Update status after each meaningful action.

sections.heading: SLA Checks
sections.text:
First response within team SLA target.
Resolution ETA updated for long-running cases.
No stale tickets without owner.

sections.heading: Troubleshooting
sections.text:
Ticket duplication : merge and preserve source references.
Unclear issue : request minimum reproducible details.
Repeated reopen : validate root cause, not only symptom fix.

sections.heading: Best Practice
sections.text: Every closure note should include what was checked, what changed, and how it was validated.

sections.heading: Introduction
sections.text: Use this page to process inbound messages submitted via contact forms.

sections.heading: Interface Reference
sections.text:
Reference screenshot for documentation purposes:
https://userguide.playagegaming.tech/img/bo/operations-and-support/7-2-3/aijygk62fkkydkbm-image-aa921b76.webp

sections.heading: Purpose
sections.text:
Capture and triage unsolicited inbound user communication.
Route messages to the right operational queue.
Prevent missed messages and response delays.

sections.heading: Triage Workflow
sections.text:
Open Support > Contact Us List .
Sort by newest and priority markers.
Classify message (payment, account, technical, legal, other).
Convert to ticket when action is required.
Mark as handled and log reference.

sections.heading: Message Classification Guide
sections.text:
Payment related : route to finance operations queue.
Account/KYC : route to compliance or account support.
Technical defect : route to support ticket + engineering follow-up.
General inquiry : respond with approved template.

sections.heading: Data Hygiene Rules
sections.text:
Remove duplicate spam entries.
Keep user contact details masked where policy requires.
Attach internal reference on every escalated item.

sections.heading: Troubleshooting
sections.text:
Unassigned backlog growing : apply auto-tag and daily triage batch.
Repeated same inquiry : publish FAQ response template internally.
No response trace : ensure ticket mapping field is mandatory.

sections.heading: Best Practice
sections.text: Run contact queue triage at fixed intervals (start, mid, end shift) instead of random checks.

sections.heading: Partner Settings
sections.text: Use this page to configure partner-level operational behavior and access boundaries.

sections.heading: Interface Reference
sections.text:
Reference screenshot for documentation purposes:
https://userguide.playagegaming.tech/img/bo/system-configuration/8-1-1/giwxwyl1kahf4cxu-image-115c3bd7.webp

sections.heading: Purpose
sections.text:
Manage partner configuration safely.
Keep partner-specific settings aligned with policy.
Prevent cross-partner scope mistakes.

sections.heading: Typical Configuration Flow
sections.text:
Open System Configuration > Partner Settings .
Select target partner entity.
Review current settings before editing.
Update only required fields.
Save and run a quick validation test.

sections.heading: Critical Checks
sections.text:
Correct partner selected.
Region/currency/payment scope matches contract.
Access boundaries do not overlap with other partners.
Audit note/comment added for change context.

sections.heading: Troubleshooting
sections.text:
Settings not applied : verify save confirmation and cache delay.
Wrong partner behavior : confirm partner ID mapping.
Unexpected permission effects : check role inheritance.

sections.heading: Best Practice
sections.text: Use change tickets and versioned notes for every partner setting update.

sections.heading: Email Template Management
sections.text: Use this module to create and maintain transactional/system email templates.

sections.heading: Interface Reference
sections.text:
Reference screenshot for documentation purposes:
https://userguide.playagegaming.tech/img/bo/system-configuration/8-1-2/ejbejjkcd1wr65iw-image-cfbebe18.webp

sections.heading: Purpose
sections.text:
Standardize outgoing communication.
Keep messaging consistent across flows.
Control template quality and localization.

sections.heading: Template Lifecycle
sections.text:
Draft template.
Add variables/placeholders.
Preview and test send.
Approve and publish.
Track and revise when needed.

sections.heading: Field Notes
sections.text:
Subject : keep short and action-oriented.
Body : use approved tone and placeholders only.
Language : maintain EN/TR parity where required.
Status : draft vs active must be explicit.

sections.heading: Validation Checklist
sections.text:
Placeholders render correctly.
Links route to expected environment.
No hardcoded personal or environment-specific data.

sections.heading: Troubleshooting
sections.text:
Broken variables : verify placeholder syntax.
Wrong language output : check template-language mapping.
No email delivered : confirm provider status and event trigger.

sections.heading: Best Practice
sections.text: Keep a small set of reusable templates and avoid one-off duplicates.

sections.heading: Bonus Management
sections.text: Use this page to configure bonus rules, eligibility, and active periods.

sections.heading: Interface Reference
sections.text:
Reference screenshot for documentation purposes:
https://userguide.playagegaming.tech/img/bo/system-configuration/8-1-3/zltfowbuwweh2ufo-image-69a1c4e8.webp

sections.heading: Purpose
sections.text:
Launch controlled bonus campaigns.
Avoid policy and payout conflicts.
Track bonus effectiveness and risk impact.

sections.heading: Configuration Flow
sections.text:
Create new bonus or edit existing one.
Define eligibility (segment, region, user type).
Set value, limits, and wagering conditions.
Configure active window (start/end).
Save and validate with test account.

sections.heading: Key Fields
sections.text:
Bonus type and value model
Min/max thresholds
Wagering or unlock conditions
Expiry and usage limit
Stacking/conflict behavior

sections.heading: Risk and Control Checks
sections.text:
Ensure bonus does not conflict with active campaigns.
Validate abuse controls and maximum exposure.
Confirm rollback behavior is defined.

sections.heading: Troubleshooting
sections.text:
Bonus not applied : check eligibility and active dates.
Unexpected overuse : review limit and duplicate claim controls.
Incorrect settlement impact : verify finance mapping.

sections.heading: Best Practice
sections.text: Enable high-impact bonuses in phased rollout instead of full instant release.

sections.heading: Ticket Event Management
sections.text: Use this page to map events to the correct ticket workflows and queues.

sections.heading: Interface Reference
sections.text:
Reference screenshot for documentation purposes:
https://userguide.playagegaming.tech/img/bo/system-configuration/8-1-4/jvogfysk8nwzwlqt-image-a498be31.webp

sections.heading: Purpose
sections.text:
Route events to the right support pipeline.
Improve SLA handling with correct priority mapping.
Avoid manual misclassification.

sections.heading: Setup Flow
sections.text:
Open System Configuration > Ticket Event Management .
Select event type.
Map priority, queue, and owner rules.
Configure notifications/escalation targets.
Save and simulate sample event.

sections.heading: What to Validate
sections.text:
Event creates ticket in expected queue.
Priority mapping is correct.
Escalation timer triggers as expected.
Duplicate event handling is controlled.

sections.heading: Troubleshooting
sections.text:
Event not creating ticket : check trigger enable status.
Wrong queue assignment : review routing condition order.
Escalation not firing : validate SLA timer config.

sections.heading: Best Practice
sections.text: Keep routing logic simple and explicit; avoid overlapping conditions.

sections.heading: Introduction
sections.text: Use this module to create, control, and audit promo code campaigns.

sections.heading: Interface Reference
sections.text:
Reference screenshot for documentation purposes:
https://userguide.playagegaming.tech/img/bo/system-configuration/8-1-5/tqdxkduq4fvnfyjq-image-b748385a.webp

sections.heading: Purpose
sections.text:
Manage code-based incentives with strict controls.
Track code usage and abuse risks.
Support campaign attribution and reporting.

sections.heading: Setup Flow
sections.text:
Create promo code set.
Define audience and redemption conditions.
Set limits (per user, total uses, date window).
Activate code batch.
Monitor redemption and deactivate when needed.

sections.heading: Control Parameters
sections.text:
Redemption cap
User-level reuse restriction
Region/currency lock
Expiry behavior
Manual override permissions

sections.heading: Validation Checklist
sections.text:
Code format and uniqueness verified.
Condition logic tested with eligible/ineligible users.
Expiry and cut-off time confirmed.

sections.heading: Troubleshooting
sections.text:
Code rejected for valid user : re-check segment mapping.
Over-redemption : verify per-user and total cap enforcement.
Campaign mismatch in reports : confirm code-to-campaign mapping.

sections.heading: Best Practice
sections.text: Use separate code pools per region/campaign to simplify tracking and rollback.

sections.heading: System Maintenance Overview
sections.text: Use this page to plan and execute controlled maintenance windows.

sections.heading: Interface Reference
sections.text:
Reference screenshot for documentation purposes:
https://userguide.playagegaming.tech/img/bo/system-configuration/8-1-6/jdvkuvyixoc5xq1i-image-9383ad3f.webp

sections.heading: Purpose
sections.text:
Protect system integrity during upgrades/fixes.
Communicate downtime clearly to users and teams.
Ensure safe start and safe rollback path.

sections.heading: Maintenance Execution Flow
sections.text:
Define scope (services/pages/regions affected).
Set start/end time and timezone.
Publish maintenance notice.
Enable maintenance mode.
Perform planned changes.
Run post-change health checks.
Disable maintenance mode and verify user access.

sections.heading: Pre-Maintenance Checklist
sections.text:
Backup/rollback plan ready.
Stakeholders informed.
Monitoring dashboard active.
On-call owners assigned.

sections.heading: Post-Maintenance Validation
sections.text:
Login and critical flows work.
Payment and support channels are operational.
Error rate and latency normalized.

sections.heading: Troubleshooting
sections.text:
Maintenance stuck enabled : verify mode toggle and cache propagation.
Users still blocked after close : clear edge cache and confirm region flags.
Unexpected error spike : trigger rollback decision process.

sections.heading: Best Practice
sections.text: Prefer low-traffic windows and always execute a short post-maintenance observation period.

sections.heading: Admin Profile Management
sections.text: Use this page to maintain core admin account details and ensure profile data stays accurate.

sections.heading: Interface Reference
sections.text:
Reference screenshot for documentation purposes:
https://userguide.playagegaming.tech/img/bo/admin-settings/9-1-1/yghzncq3gveke5nw-embedded-image-cnewluow-2ae96c9f.webp

sections.heading: Purpose
sections.text:
Keep admin identity and contact details up to date.
Ensure account ownership and responsibility are clear.
Prevent operational confusion caused by outdated profile data.

sections.heading: Typical Update Flow
sections.text:
Open Admin Settings > Profile .
Review current profile fields.
Update editable fields (name, contact, optional metadata).
Save changes.
Re-open profile page to verify persistence.

sections.heading: Field Validation Checklist
sections.text:
Display name follows internal naming policy.
Contact details are reachable and active.
Role/department labels are correct for audit context.

sections.heading: When to Update Profile
sections.text:
Role or team change
Contact change (email/phone)
Ownership transfer for operational responsibilities

sections.heading: Troubleshooting
sections.text:
Changes not saved : verify required fields and retry.
Profile data reverted : check if another admin update overwrote changes.
Read-only field confusion : confirm role permissions for editable fields.

sections.heading: Best Practice
sections.text: Review profile data monthly for all high-privilege admin accounts.

sections.heading: Two-Factor Authentication
sections.text: Use this page to configure and validate 2FA for admin sign-in security.

sections.heading: Interface Reference
sections.text:
Reference screenshot for documentation purposes:
https://userguide.playagegaming.tech/img/bo/admin-settings/9-1-2/lllqc1s99cigahwx-embedded-image-mx83bimh-425abf8d.webp

sections.heading: Purpose
sections.text:
Protect admin access beyond password-only login.
Reduce account takeover risk.
Enforce compliance for privileged users.

sections.heading: Enable/Update Flow
sections.text:
Open Admin Settings > Two-Factor Authentication .
Choose preferred method (authenticator app, OTP channel, if available).
Complete setup verification code.
Save and confirm 2FA status is active.
Test one fresh login.

sections.heading: Recovery and Safety Controls
sections.text:
Store backup/recovery codes securely.
Keep device time synchronized for OTP validity.
Rotate device binding if mobile device changes.

sections.heading: Validation Checklist
sections.text:
2FA prompt appears at next login.
Invalid OTP attempts are blocked.
Backup/recovery path works for authorized user.

sections.heading: Troubleshooting
sections.text:
OTP rejected repeatedly : sync device time and request new code.
No 2FA prompt : verify enforcement policy for current role.
Device lost : trigger recovery flow via authorized admin process.

sections.heading: Best Practice
sections.text: Require 2FA for all admin roles and audit exceptions monthly.

sections.heading: App Settings
sections.text: Use this page to configure personal admin application preferences.

sections.heading: Interface Reference
sections.text:
Reference screenshot for documentation purposes:
https://userguide.playagegaming.tech/img/bo/admin-settings/9-1-3/r8cg9bwy8icbxmnd-image-65de112c.webp

sections.heading: Purpose
sections.text:
Improve daily usability without changing global system behavior.
Keep personal preference settings consistent.
Reduce navigation and handling friction during operations.

sections.heading: Common Settings Area
sections.text:
Language preference
Theme or display options
Notification preferences
Session behavior options (where allowed)

sections.heading: Update Flow
sections.text:
Open Admin Settings > App Settings .
Update required preferences.
Save settings.
Refresh page or re-login if prompted.
Validate changes are applied.

sections.heading: Validation Checklist
sections.text:
UI language/theme reflects saved values.
Notifications follow selected preference.
Settings persist after re-login.

sections.heading: Troubleshooting
sections.text:
Settings reset after login : check profile sync or browser storage restrictions.
Option not visible : role or feature flag may hide it.
Unexpected behavior : clear cache and reapply settings.

sections.heading: Best Practice
sections.text: Keep personal settings minimal and consistent to avoid shift-to-shift confusion.

sections.heading: Logout
sections.text: Use this flow to close admin sessions safely and avoid unauthorized reuse.

sections.heading: Interface Reference
sections.text:
Reference screenshot for documentation purposes:
https://userguide.playagegaming.tech/img/bo/admin-settings/9-1-4/5iidpxhdg4logd2n-image-63e3f389.webp

sections.heading: Purpose
sections.text:
End active session securely.
Prevent access from shared or unattended devices.
Ensure clean transition between admins on operational machines.

sections.heading: Logout Flow
sections.text:
Open profile/menu area.
Click Logout/Sign Out .
Confirm action (if confirmation modal appears).
Verify redirect to login page.

sections.heading: Post-Logout Validation
sections.text:
Back button does not reopen authenticated pages.
Refreshing old BO URL requires login.
Session token is invalidated.

sections.heading: Troubleshooting
sections.text:
Still logged in after logout : clear browser cache/cookies and retry.
Redirect loop to dashboard : verify session token invalidation.
Multiple tab issue : close all open BO tabs after sign out.

sections.heading: Best Practice
sections.text: Always logout before leaving your workstation, even for short breaks.
